aesthetic: The Liber Novus — an illuminated mandala on lapis-and-gold vellum
anchor: Jung's own hand-painted Red Book (Liber Novus, 1915-1930) mandala folios — deep lapis ultramarine, gold leaf, cinnabar, malachite, iron-gall ink on parchment — fused with the alchemical squaring-of-the-circle emblem (circle, square, quaternity) Jung read as the projected drama of individuation.
rationale: This corpus's load-bearing claim is that the self is a mandala whose center cannot be told apart from the God-image (CW 11, para 231), and that individuation is the squaring of the circle — making the threefold whole by integrating the dark fourth. The native artifact is therefore Jung's own illuminated manuscript: the painted mandala, the quaternity, the two fishes of the Piscean aeon, the alchemical coniunctio. Lapis = the unconscious depth; cinnabar = the numinous center and the Red Book's fire; gold = the imago Dei; malachite = alchemy; aubergine = spirit and the missing fourth.

fonts.display: Baskerville, 'Baskerville Old Face', 'Big Caslon', 'Hoefler Text', Garamond, Georgia, serif
fonts.body: Palatino, 'Palatino Linotype', 'Book Antiqua', Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif
fonts.mono: 'Courier New', Consolas, 'DejaVu Sans Mono', ui-monospace, monospace
